title: Passive Voice
subtitle: Voz Passiva

introduction.definition: A voz passiva é quando o sujeito RECEBE a ação, não a pratica.
introduction.structure: Subject + be + Past Participle (+ by + agent)
introduction.example: The lesson is explained by the teacher.
introduction.note: A voz passiva transfere o foco do agente (quem faz) para o objeto (quem recebe).

basicConcept.title: O Conceito Básico
basicConcept.whatIs: Na voz passiva, o sujeito recebe a ação.

components.subject: Quem recebe a ação
components.pastParticiple: Forma do verbo em past participle

example.sentence: An email is written by the student.
example.subject: An email (quem recebe)

whenToUse.example: My phone was stolen. (Não sabemos quem roubou)
The email was sent yesterday. (Óbvio: por alguém)

whenToUse.situation: Quando o agente é menos importante que a ação
whenToUse.example: The project was completed on time. (Importa que foi completado, não quem fez)
The experiment was conducted by researchers.

whenToUse.situation: Quando queremos ser discreto sobre quem faz
whenToUse.example: Mistakes were made. (Não queremos dizer exatamente quem errou)

steps.title: Identifique o objeto (o que recebe a ação)
steps.example: The teacher explains the lesson. → object: the lesson

steps.title: Este objeto vira sujeito na passiva
Use: be + past participle do verbo original
(Opcional) Adicione by + agente original
steps.example: The lesson is explained by the teacher.

example.passive: The meal is prepared by the chef.

allTenses.passiveStructure: Object + am/is/are + past participle + by + agent
allTenses.passiveExample: English is taught by the teacher.

allTenses.passiveStructure: Object + am/is/are + being + past participle + by + agent
allTenses.passiveExample: English is being taught by the teacher.
allTenses.note: Adiciona 'being' entre be e past participle

allTenses.passiveStructure: Object + have/has + been + past participle + by + agent
allTenses.activeExample: The chef has cooked the meal.
allTenses.passiveExample: The meal has been cooked by the chef.

allTenses.passiveStructure: Object + was/were + past participle + by + agent
allTenses.passiveExample: The criminal was arrested by the police.

allTenses.passiveStructure: Object + was/were + being + past participle + by + agent
allTenses.passiveExample: English was being taught by the teacher.

allTenses.passiveStructure: Object + had + been + past participle + by + agent
allTenses.activeExample: The chef had cooked the meal.
allTenses.passiveExample: The meal had been cooked by the chef.

allTenses.passiveStructure: Object + will + be + past participle + by + agent
allTenses.passiveExample: English will be taught by the teacher.

allTenses.activeStructure: Subject + will + have + past participle + Object
allTenses.passiveStructure: Object + will + have + been + past participle + by + agent
allTenses.activeExample: The chef will have cooked the meal.
allTenses.passiveExample: The meal will have been cooked by the chef.

byAgentUsage.title: Uso de 'by + Agent'
whenToInclude: Quando o agente é importante ou novo na informação
whenToOmit: Quando o agente é óbvio (people, someone)
Quando o agente é geral (society, tradition)

examples.include: This painting was created by Picasso.

twoObjectsProblem.title: Verbos com Dois Objetos
twoObjectsProblem.explanation: Alguns verbos têm dois objetos. Qual vira sujeito?

verbsWithTwoObjects.passiveOption1: Mary was given a present by John.
verbsWithTwoObjects.passiveOption2: A present was given to Mary by John.

verbsWithTwoObjects.active: The company sent employees a letter.
verbsWithTwoObjects.passiveOption1: Employees were sent a letter by the company.
verbsWithTwoObjects.passiveOption2: A letter was sent to the employees by the company.

verbsWithTwoObjects.passiveOption1: Students were taught English by the professor.
verbsWithTwoObjects.passiveOption2: English was taught to students by the professor.

twoObjectsProblem.note: A opção 1 (indirect object) é mais comum e natural.

verbsNotPassive.title: Verbos que NÃO Têm Voz Passiva
verbsNotPassive.reason: Verbos intransitivos (que não têm objeto) nunca podem ser passivos.

commonErrors.error: The lesson was explained to the students by the teacher.
commonErrors.correct: The lesson was explained by the teacher to the students.
commonErrors.explanation: By + agent vem depois de be + past participle

commonErrors.error: English is speak in many countries.
commonErrors.correct: English is being spoken in many countries.
commonErrors.explanation: Present continuous passive: be + being + past participle

commonErrors.error: The meal has prepared by the chef.
commonErrors.correct: The meal has been prepared by the chef.
commonErrors.explanation: Present perfect passive: have/has + been + past participle

commonErrors.error: This car was make in Japan.
commonErrors.correct: This car was made in Japan.

passiveVsActiveComparison.passive: The project was completed by the students.
passiveVsActiveComparison.difference: Foco muda do agente para a ação/resultado

passiveVsActiveComparison.passive: This article was written by Sarah.
passiveVsActiveComparison.difference: Enfase: o artigo é mais importante que Sarah
A lei é o foco, o governo é menos importante

keyPoints: Passive voice transfere o foco do agente para o objeto.
Use quando o agente é desconhecido, óbvio ou menos importante.
Apenas verbos transitivos podem ser passivos.
A ordem: sujeito + be + past participle + by + agent
